Finding: Strict v4.1 experiments must bind an immutable policy; legacy v2 fallback is no longer silently accepted in a strict binding.
Resolution / next action: Policy registry + checksum/schema binding; legacy fallback retained only for backwards-compatible non-strict scripts.

Finding: Historical policy-training dynamics did not expose v4 runtime cognition.
Resolution / next action: Native 32-feature v4.1 training environment now includes persistent pbest, HPEM, contextual credit, variable intelligence, adaptive epsilon, dual-lane readiness and recovery semantics. A single shared transition implementation remains future work.

Finding: The policy could not observe important v4 memory/precision states.
Resolution / next action: Native v4.1 state adds HPEM occupancy, consensus, readiness, success-memory density, learning-lane fraction, precision state/radius and variable-group concentration.

Finding: No formal policy qualification gate existed.
Resolution / next action: Candidate vs reference vs No-AI paired qualification, immutable SHA provenance, grades, paired evidence, and qualification history are implemented.

Finding: Training completion still does not automatically select the best independently qualified checkpoint across a long training lineage.
Resolution / next action: Policy lineage/indefinite checkpoint evolution is intentionally reserved for v5.0; v4.1 registers each completed checkpoint as a candidate requiring qualification.

Finding: The bundled legacy policy has no demonstrated native-v4.1 ORPD qualification.
Resolution / next action: The UI labels it legacy/unqualified until the user runs a recorded qualification. No superiority claim is made.

Finding: AI/rule/online-credit authority weights are not yet empirically calibrated as universally optimal.
Resolution / next action: Use paired development-case ablation before changing frozen defaults; IEEE 118/300 remain protected holdouts.

Finding: CALO does not yet use the same fully Torch-native optimizer-control path as baseline Torch optimizers.
Resolution / next action: Scientific evaluator remains accelerator-native; a complete CUDA/XPU CALO-control rewrite requires strict seeded parity tests and is deferred rather than risking equation drift.

Finding: CALO cognitive/memory control state remains primarily compact NumPy host state.
Resolution / next action: v4.1 reduces host overhead and records control/evaluator timing, but does not falsely claim fully device-resident CALO control.

Finding: Per-learner candidate decisions still contain Python-level logic.
Resolution / next action: v4.1 removes repeated per-learner best/mean/variable-schema construction while preserving the frozen seeded peer-sampling sequence; complete grouped operator kernels remain future work.

Finding: CUDA policy inference still materializes small action arrays on the host for NumPy CALO control.
Resolution / next action: Eliminating this transfer depends on the future fully device-resident control path.

Finding: Microbatch warm-up looked like end-to-end CALO throughput although it measured only the evaluator.
Resolution / next action: GUI/log wording now explicitly says evaluator-only; CALO result metadata separately reports policy, candidate-generation, evaluator, and learning-update timing.

Finding: Persistent exact-evaluation caching could cost more than it saved at very low cross-batch hit rates.
Resolution / next action: Within-batch exact dedup always remains; persistent cross-batch storage auto-disables after sufficient evidence when its hit rate is below threshold.

Finding: HPEM/context/success-memory operations include small host-side loops.
Resolution / next action: Bounded memories keep cost small and 3D scratch is reused; further device kernels require parity-qualified future work.

Finding: Cross-run policy broker may add latency for an isolated single CALO run.
Resolution / next action: Retain broker for multi-run batching; add a measured direct-inference fast path only after end-to-end profiling proves benefit.

Finding: Workflow completion state was not restored.
Resolution / next action: Workflow snapshots are persisted; older experiments without snapshots infer completed setup stages from authoritative experiment existence.

Finding: Old setup parameter widgets did not fully rehydrate.
Resolution / next action: Power-system, algorithm, robust-scenario and CALO policy/intelligence loaders now restore from the saved ExperimentConfig; connected panels refresh from config_changed.

Finding: CALO Intelligence did not restore the exact experiment policy/intelligence selection.
Resolution / next action: Experiment policy ID/path/SHA/schema and historical-learning controls are restored from immutable config/binding.

Finding: LiveOptimizationPanel.load_experiment existed but was not wired into resume/open.
Resolution / next action: Central ExperimentWorkspaceRestorer now invokes it for resume and historical workspace opening.

Finding: Previously completed convergence graphs were not automatically reconstructed.
Resolution / next action: Stored numeric run histories rebuild live/convergence views; no screenshot is treated as scientific source data.

Finding: A crash mid-run does not yet persist every downsampled live-telemetry point for exact visual reconstruction before the latest committed run checkpoint.
Resolution / next action: Deep run-checkpoint/telemetry continuation belongs to the planned v5.0 continuation architecture.

Finding: Selected run/plot/view state was lost.
Resolution / next action: Lightweight Live Optimization view state and last workspace are persisted separately from scientific data and restored on reopen.
